Recommendation: Ensure to create Machine Learning Compute resources in secondary region
Best Practices Guidance: Create compute resources for training a Machine Learning model in selected regions. Ensure both regions have sufficient compute quota for dynamic scaling. Customers must configure HA across zones for attached compute resources like AKS, Azure Databricks, Container Instances.

Recommendation: Service should disable public network access
Best Practices Guidance: Service should disable public network access

Recommendation: Choose SKUs with longer terms and avoid those nearing retirement
Best Practices Guidance: When choosing SKUs, opt for those that support longer terms and steer clear of any SKUs that are nearing retirement. This ensures that you can continue to use the SKU for a longer period of time.

Recommendation: Deploy Azure Machine learning workspace in secondary region
Best Practices Guidance: If your primary workspace is unavailable, switch to the secondary workspace to continue work. Azure Machine Learning doesn't auto-submit jobs to the secondary workspace during an outage. Update your code configuration to point to the new workspace resource.

Recommendation: Service should have private enpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Service should have private enpoints enabled

Recommendation: Plan for a multi-regional deployment of Azure Machine Learning and associated resources
Best Practices Guidance: Ensure you have a recovery strategy defined. Check regional availability and paired regions. Machine Learning doesn't have auto failover. Therefore, you must design a strategy that encompasses the workspace and all its dependencies, such as Key Vault, Azure Storage, and Container Registry.

Recommendation: Ensure checkpoints are used for AI training models
Best Practices Guidance: Checkpoint optimization for large model training is crucial for disaster recovery. It reduces training time, increases reliability, improves cost efficiency, enhances resource utilization, and supports scalability by saving model states periodically to resume training from the last saved point.

Recommendation: Service should have diagnostic settings enabled
Selecting regions for BCDR, ensure that both regions offer adequate compute quotas
Best Practices Guidance: When selecting regions for BCDR, ensure that both regions offer adequate compute quotas to meet your requirements in the same SKU. This ensures that you can failover to the secondary region without any issues.

Recommendation: Avoid NC and NC_Promo series Azure VMs for machine learning quotas; migrate to newer versions
Best Practices Guidance: Avoid selecting NC and NC_Promo series Azure virtual machines for machine learning VM quotas and make sure to migrate to newer versions.

Recommendation: Make Azure Machine Learning quota requests through the Azure Machine Learning Studio
Best Practices Guidance: Requests for additional Azure Machine Learning quota should be made through the Azure Machine Learning Studio instead of the subscription level in the Azure portal.

Recommendation: PostgreSQL should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: PostgreSQL should have private endpoints enabled

Recommendation: PostgreSQL should enforce SSL
Best Practices Guidance: PostgreSQL should enforce SSL

Recommendation: PostgreSQL should enforce TLS >= 1.2
Best Practices Guidance: PostgreSQL should enforce TLS >= 1.2

Recommendation: PostgreSQL should have diagnostic settings enabled
Enable Virtual Network Flow Logs
Best Practices Guidance: Improves monitoring and security for Azure and Hybrid connectivity

Recommendation: Virtual Networks without subnets
Best Practices Guidance: Virtual Networks without subnets.

Recommendation: Virtual Network should have at least two DNS servers assigned
Best Practices Guidance: Virtual Network should have at least two DNS servers assigned

Recommendation: Virtual Network should have diagnostic settings enabled
ContainerApp should use Azure Files to persist container data
Best Practices Guidance: ContainerApps should use Azure Files for persistent storage to ensure data durability and availability across container restarts.

Recommendation: ContainerApp should avoid using session affinity
Best Practices Guidance:
ContainerApps should avoid using sticky sessions (session affinity)
as it can impact load distribution and high availability. Design applications
to be stateless when possible.

Recommendation: ContainerApp should not allow insecure ingress traffic
Best Practices Guidance: ContainerApps should not allow insecure HTTP traffic. All ingress traffic should use HTTPS to ensure data is encrypted in transit.

Recommendation: ContainerApp should use Managed Identities
Best Practices Guidance: ContainerApps should use Managed Identities to authenticate with Azure services instead of storing credentials in code or configuration.

Recommendation: Event Grid Domain should have local authentication disabled
Best Practices Guidance: Event Grid Domain should have local authentication disabled

Recommendation: Event Grid Domain should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Event Grid Domain should have private endpoints enabled

Recommendation: Event Grid Domain should have diagnostic settings enabled
Azure Database for MySQL - Flexible Server should have diagnostic settings enabled
Azure Database for MySQL - Flexible Server should have private access enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Database for MySQL - Flexible Server should have private access enabled

Recommendation: Enable HA with zone redundancy
Best Practices Guidance: Enable HA with zone redundancy on flexible server instances to deploy a standby replica in a different zone, offering automatic failover capability for improved reliability and disaster recovery.

Recommendation: Configure geo redundant backup storage
Best Practices Guidance: Configure GRS to ensure that your database meets its availability and durability targets even in the face of failures or disasters.

Recommendation: Configure one or more read replicas
Best Practices Guidance: Configure one or more read replicas to ensure that your database meets its availability and durability targets even in the face of failures or disasters.

Recommendation: Configure storage auto-grow
Best Practices Guidance: Configure storage auto-grow to prevent the server from running out of storage and becoming read-only.

Recommendation: Virtual Network Gateway should have availability zones enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Virtual Network Gateway should use zone-redundant SKUs (SKUs ending with "AZ") to provide high availability across availability zones.

Recommendation: Configure customer-controlled VPN gateway maintenance
Best Practices Guidance: VPN gateways are updated for improved functionality, reliability, performance, and security. Customer-controlled maintenance configuration and scheduling minimize update impact and align with your maintenance windows.

Recommendation: Choose a Zone-redundant VPN gateway
Best Practices Guidance: Deploying zone-redundant virtual network gateways across availability zones ensures zone-resiliency, improving access to mission-critical, scalable services on Azure. Mission Critical workloads should use dual ExpressRoutes instead of VPN.

Recommendation: Enable Active-Active VPN Gateways for redundancy
Best Practices Guidance: The active-active mode is available for all SKUs except Basic, allowing for two Gateway IP configurations and two public IP addresses, enhancing redundancy and traffic handling. Mission Critical workloads should use dual ExpressRoutes instead of VPN.

Recommendation: Use Zone-redundant ExpressRoute gateway SKUs
Best Practices Guidance: Azure ExpressRoute gateway offers variable SLAs based on deployment in single or multiple availability zones. To deploy virtual network gateways across zones automatically, use zone-redundant gateways for accessing critical, scalable services with increased resilience.

Recommendation: Virtual Network Gateways without Point-to-site configuration or Connections
Best Practices Guidance: Virtual Network Gateways without Point-to-site configuration or Connections.

Recommendation: Deploy VPN gateways with zone-redundant Public IPs
Best Practices Guidance: For zone-redundant VPN gateways, always use zone-redundant Standard SKU public IPs to avoid deploying all instances in one zone. This ensures the gateway's reliability. Mission Critical workloads should use dual ExpressRoutes instead of VPN.

Recommendation: Connect ExpressRoute gateway with circuits from diverse peering locations
Best Practices Guidance: For improved reliability, each ExpressRoute gateway should connect to at least two circuits, with each circuit sourced from a different peering location. This setup ensures diverse connectivity paths, enhancing resilience and minimizing service disruption risks.

Recommendation: Configure customer-controlled ExpressRoute gateway maintenance
Best Practices Guidance: ExpressRoute gateways are updated for improved functionality, reliability, performance, and security. Customer-controlled maintenance configuration and scheduling minimize update impact and align with your maintenance windows.

Recommendation: Virtual Network Gateway should have diagnostic settings enabled
Azure Data Factory should have diagnostic settings enabled
Convert Classic Deployments
Best Practices Guidance: Classic Application Insights retires in February 2024. To minimize disruption to existing application monitoring scenarios, transition to workspace-based Application Insights before 29 February 2024.

Recommendation: Availability Sets not associated to any VM or VMSS
Best Practices Guidance: Availability Sets not associated to any VM or VMSS.

Recommendation: Monitor Memory Utilization to ensure sufficient resources for workloads
Best Practices Guidance: Ensure sufficient memory resources to prevent host resource exhaustion in Azure VMware Solution. It uses vSphere DRS and vSphere HA for dynamic workload management. Yet, continuous memory use over 95% leads to disk swapping, affecting workloads.

Recommendation: Enable Stretched Clusters for Multi-AZ Availability of the vSAN Datastore
Best Practices Guidance: For Azure VMware Solution, enabling Stretched Clusters offers 99.99% SLA, synchronous storage replication (RPO=0), and spreads vSAN datastore across two AZs. Must be done at initial setup, needing double quota due to extension across AZs.

Recommendation: Configure Azure Monitor Alert warning thresholds for vSAN datastore utilization
Best Practices Guidance: Ensure VMware vSAN datastore slack space is maintained for SLA by monitoring storage utilization and setting alerts at 70% and 75% utilization to allow for capacity planning. To expand, add hosts or external storage like Azure Elastic SAN, Azure NetApp Files, if CPU and RAM requirements are met.

Recommendation: Monitor CPU Utilization to ensure sufficient resources for workloads
Best Practices Guidance: Ensure sufficient compute resources to avoid host resource exhaustion in Azure VMware Solution, which utilizes vSphere DRS and HA for dynamic workload resource management. However, sustained CPU utilization over 95% may increase CPU Ready times, impacting workloads.

Recommendation: Enable Cross Region Restore for your GRS Recovery Services Vault
Best Practices Guidance: Cross Region Restore enables the restoration of Azure VMs in a secondary, Azure paired region, facilitating drills for audit or compliance and allowing recovery of VMs or disks in the event of a primary region disaster. It is an opt-in feature available exclusively for GRS vaults.

Recommendation: Enable Soft Delete for Recovery Services Vaults in Azure Backup
Best Practices Guidance: With soft delete, if backup data is deleted, the backup data is retained for 14 additional days, allowing the recovery of that backup item with no data loss with no cost to you. Soft delete is enabled by default. Disabling this feature isn't recommended.

Recommendation: Migrate from classic alerts to built-in Azure Monitor alerts for Azure Recovery Services Vaults
Best Practices Guidance: Classic alerts for Recovery Services vaults in Azure Backup will be retired on 31 March 2026.

Recommendation: Monitor changes in Route Tables with Azure Monitor
Best Practices Guidance: Create Alerts with Azure Monitor for operations like Create or Update Route Table to spot unauthorized/undesired changes in production resources. This setup aids in identifying improper routing changes, including efforts to evade firewalls or access resources from outside.

Recommendation: Route Tables not attached to any subnet
Best Practices Guidance: Route Tables not attached to any subnet.

Recommendation: SQL should enforce TLS >= 1.2
Best Practices Guidance: SQL servers should enforce TLS 1.2 or higher to ensure secure encrypted connections to the database.

Recommendation: SQL should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: SQL servers should have private endpoints enabled to secure database access and prevent exposure to the public internet.

Recommendation: Azure Data Explorer Production Cluster should not use Dev SKU
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Data Explorer Production Cluster should not use Dev SKU

Recommendation: Azure Data Explorer should have diagnostic settings enabled
Azure Data Explorer should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Data Explorer should have private endpoints enabled

Recommendation: Azure Data Explorer should use Disk Encryption
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Data Explorer should use Disk Encryption

Recommendation: Azure Data Explorer should use Managed Identities
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Data Explorer should use Managed Identities

Recommendation: Implement rate-limiting across ExpressRoute Direct Circuits to optimize network flow
Best Practices Guidance: Rate limiting controls traffic volume between on-premises networks and Azure via ExpressRoute Direct, applying to private or Microsoft peering. It distributes port bandwidth, ensures stability, and prevents congestion, with steps outlined for enabling on circuits.

Recommendation: Ensure zone redundancy is enabled in supported regions
Best Practices Guidance: When using the Azure portal, zone redundancy is automatically enabled. However, some Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools may default this to false. To ensure replication of metadata and events across data centers in an availability zone, always verify that zone redundancy is enabled.

Recommendation: Enable auto-inflate on Event Hub Standard tier
Best Practices Guidance: Enable auto-inflate on Event Hub Standard tier namespaces to automatically scale up throughput units (TUs), meeting usage needs and preventing data ingress or egress throttle scenarios by adjusting to allowed rates.

Recommendation: Event Hub Namespace should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Event Hub Namespace should have private endpoints enabled

Recommendation: Event Hub should have local authentication disabled
Best Practices Guidance: Event Hub should have local authentication disabled

Recommendation: Event Hub Namespace should have diagnostic settings enabled
Key vaults should have soft delete enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Key Vault's soft-delete feature enables recovery of deleted vaults and objects like keys, secrets, and certificates. When enabled, marked resources are retained for 90 days, allowing for their recovery, essentially undoing deletion.

Recommendation: Key vaults should have purge protection enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Purge protection secures against malicious deletions by enforcing a retention period for soft deleted key vaults, ensuring no one, not even insiders or Microsoft, can purge your key vaults during this period, preventing permanent data loss.

Recommendation: Key Vault should have diagnostic settings enabled
Ensure Time-To-Live (TTL) is set appropriately to ensure RTOs can be met
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Private DNS allows the Time-To-Live (TTL) for record sets in the zone to be set to a value between 1 and 2147483647 seconds. You should ensure that the TTL for the DNS record sets in your DNS Zones are set appropriately to meet your RTO targets.

Recommendation: Private DNS zones without Virtual Network Links
Best Practices Guidance: Private DNS zones without Virtual Network Links.

Recommendation: Configure host pool scheduled agent updates
Best Practices Guidance: Create up to two maintenance windows for the Azure Virtual Desktop agent, side-by-side stack, and Geneva Monitoring agent to get updated so that updates don't happen during peak business hours.

Recommendation: IP Groups not attached to any Azure Firewall
Best Practices Guidance: IP Groups not attached to any Azure Firewall.

Recommendation: Azure Databricks should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Databricks should have private endpoints enabled

Recommendation: Azure Databricks should have the Public IP disabled
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Databricks should have the Public IP disabled

Recommendation: Azure Databricks should have diagnostic settings enabled
Ensure ODAA infrastructure is in Available state under normal operations
Best Practices Guidance:
ODAA Exadata infrastructures can be in a number of states during its lifecycle (Available, Failed, Maintenance In Progress, Provisioning, Terminated, Terminating and Updating).
Ensure that the infrastructure is in Available state under normal operations.

Recommendation: Use Standard SKU and Zone-Redundant IPs when applicable
Best Practices Guidance: Public IP addresses in Azure can be of standard SKU, available as non-zonal, zonal, or zone-redundant. Zone-redundant IPs are accessible across all zones, resisting any single zone failure, thereby providing higher resilience.

Recommendation: Use NAT gateway for outbound connectivity to avoid SNAT Exhaustion
Best Practices Guidance: Prevent connectivity failures due to SNAT port exhaustion by employing NAT gateway for outbound traffic from virtual networks, ensuring dynamic scaling and secure internet connections.

Recommendation: Upgrade Basic SKU public IP addresses to Standard SKU
Best Practices Guidance: Basic SKU public IP addresses will be retired on September 30, 2025. Users are advised to upgrade to Standard SKU public IP addresses before this date to avoid service disruptions.

Recommendation: Public IPs not attached to any resource
Best Practices Guidance: Public IPs not attached to any resource.

Recommendation: Configure one or more read replicas
Best Practices Guidance: Configure one or more read replicas to ensure that your database meets its availability and durability targets even in the face of failures or disasters.

Recommendation: Configure storage auto-grow
Best Practices Guidance: Configure storage auto-grow to prevent the server from running out of storage and becoming read-only.

Recommendation: Enable HA with zone redundancy
Best Practices Guidance: Enable HA with zone redundancy on flexible server instances to deploy a standby replica in a different zone, offering automatic failover capability for improved reliability and disaster recovery.

Recommendation: Enable custom maintenance schedule
Best Practices Guidance: Use custom maintenance schedule on flexible server instances to select a preferred time for service updates to be applied.

Recommendation: Configure geo redundant backup storage
Best Practices Guidance: Configure GRS to ensure that your database meets its availability and durability targets even in the face of failures or disasters.

Recommendation: PostgreSQL should have private access enabled
Best Practices Guidance: PostgreSQL should have private access enabled

Recommendation: PostgreSQL should have diagnostic settings enabled
SignalR should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: SignalR should have private endpoints enabled

Recommendation: Enable zone redundancy for SignalR
Best Practices Guidance: Use SignalR with zone redundancy for production to improve uptime. This feature, available in the Premium tier, is activated upon creating or upgrading to Premium. Standard can upgrade to Premium without downtime.

Recommendation: SignalR should have diagnostic settings enabled
Set minimum instance count to 2 for app service
Best Practices Guidance: App Service should be configured with a minimum of two instances for production workloads. If apps have a longer warmup time a minimum of three instances should be used.

Recommendation: Migrate App Service to availability Zone Support
Best Practices Guidance: Azure's feature of deploying App Service plans across availability zones enhances resiliency and reliability by ensuring operation during datacenter failures, providing redundancy without needing different regions, thus minimizing downtime and maintaining uninterrupted services.

Recommendation: App Service plans without hosting Apps
Best Practices Guidance: App Service plans without hosting Apps.

Recommendation: Use Standard or Premium tier
Best Practices Guidance: Choose Standard/Premium Azure App Service Plan for robust apps with advanced scaling, high availability, better performance, and multiple slots, ensuring resilience and continuous operation.

Recommendation: Plan should have diagnostic settings enabled
Azure Database for MySQL - Single Server is on the retirement path
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Database for MySQL - Single Server is on the retirement path

Recommendation: Azure Database for MySQL - Single Server should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Database for MySQL - Single Server should have private endpoints enabled

Recommendation: Azure Database for MySQL - Single Server should have diagnostic settings enabled
Service Bus should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Service Bus should have private endpoints enabled

Recommendation: Service Bus should have local authentication disabled
Best Practices Guidance: Service Bus should have local authentication disabled

Recommendation: Service Bus should have diagnostic settings enabled
Configure the minimum TLS version for Service Bus namespaces to TLS v1.2 or higher
Best Practices Guidance: As of 31 October 2024, TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 will no longer be supported on Azure including Service Bus to enhance security and provide best-in-class encryption for your data. Change the minimum TLS version for your Service Bus namespace to TLS v1.2 or higher.

Recommendation: SQL elastic pool without databases
Best Practices Guidance: SQL elastic pool without databases.

Recommendation: Do not create more than 4000 Citrix VDA servers per subscription
Best Practices Guidance: A Citrix Managed Azure subscription supports VMs with VDA for app/desktop delivery, excluding other machines like Cloud Connectors. When close to the limit, signaled by a dashboard notification, and with sufficient licenses, request another subscription. Can't exceed the given limits for catalogs.

Recommendation: Configure Service Health Alerts
Best Practices Guidance: Service health gives a personalized health view of Azure services and regions used, offering the best place for notifications on outages, planned maintenance, and health advisories by knowing the services used.

Recommendation: Use maintenance configurations for the Dedicated and/or Isolated VM SKUs
Best Practices Guidance: The maintenance configuration settings let users schedule and manage updates, making sure the updates or interruptions on the VM are performed within a planned timeframe.

Recommendation: Migrate VMs using availability sets to VMSS Flex
Best Practices Guidance: While availability sets are not scheduled for immediate deprecation, they are planned to be deprecated in the future. Migrate workloads from VMs to VMSS Flex for deployment across zones or within the same zone across different fault domains (FDs) for better reliability.

Recommendation: Reserve Compute Capacity for critical workloads
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Capacity Reservations ensure high availability for virtual machines by reserving compute capacity in advance within a specific region or availability zone. This guarantees that VMs will have the necessary resources during peak demand or maintenance events, enhancing reliability and uptime.

Recommendation: Enable Accelerated Networking (AccelNet)
Best Practices Guidance: Accelerated networking enables SR-IOV to a VM, greatly improving its networking performance by bypassing the host from the data path, which reduces latency, jitter, and CPU utilization for demanding network workloads on supported VM types.

Recommendation: Enable VM Insights
Best Practices Guidance: VM Insights monitors VM and scale set performance, health, running processes, and dependencies. It enhances the predictability of application performance and availability by pinpointing performance bottlenecks and network issues, and it clarifies if problems are related to other dependencies.

Recommendation: Use Managed Disks for VM disks
Best Practices Guidance: Azure is retiring unmanaged disks on September 30, 2025. Users should plan the migration to avoid disruptions and maintain service reliability.

Recommendation: Replicate VMs using Azure Site Recovery
Best Practices Guidance: Replicating Azure VMs via Site Recovery entails continuous, asynchronous disk replication to a target region. Recovery points are generated every few minutes, ensuring a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) in minutes.

Recommendation: Backup VMs with Azure Backup service
Best Practices Guidance: Enable backups for your virtual machines with Azure Backup to secure and quickly recover your data. This service offers simple, secure, and cost-effective solutions for backing up and recovering data from the Microsoft Azure cloud.

Recommendation: Validate VM functionality with a Site Recovery test failover to check performance at target
Best Practices Guidance: Perform a test failover to validate your BCDR strategy and ensure that your applications are functioning correctly in the target region without impacting your production environment. Test your Disaster Recovery plan periodically without any data loss or downtime, using test failovers.

Recommendation: Don't use A or B-Series VMs for production needing constant full CPU performance
Best Practices Guidance: A-series VMs are tailored for entry-level workloads like development and testing, including use cases such as development and test servers, low traffic web servers, and small to medium databases.

Recommendation: Run production workloads on two or more VMs using VMSS Flex
Best Practices Guidance: Production VM workloads should be deployed on multiple VMs and grouped in a VMSS Flex instance to intelligently distribute across the platform, minimizing the impact of platform faults and updates.

Recommendation: Deploy VMs across Availability Zones
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Availability Zones, within each Azure region, are tolerant to local failures, protecting applications and data against unlikely Datacenter failures by being physically separate.

Recommendation: Mission Critical Workloads should consider using Premium or Ultra Disks
Best Practices Guidance: Compared to Standard HDD and SSD, Premium SSD, SSD v2, and Ultra Disks offer improved performance, configurability, and higher single-instance VM uptime SLAs. The lowest SLA of all disks on a VM applies, so it is best to use Premium or Ultra Disks for the highest uptime SLA.

Recommendation: IP Forwarding should only be enabled for Network Virtual Appliances
Best Practices Guidance: IP forwarding allows a virtual machine network interface to receive and send network traffic not destined for or originating from its assigned IP addresses.

Recommendation: When AccelNet is enabled, you must manually update the GuestOS NIC driver
Best Practices Guidance: When Accelerated Networking is enabled, the default Azure VNet interface in GuestOS is swapped for a Mellanox, and its driver comes from a 3rd party. Marketplace images have the latest Mellanox drivers, but post-deployment, updating the driver is the user's responsibility.

Recommendation: Virtual WAN should have diagnostic settings enabled
MariaDB should have diagnostic settings enabled
Deploy AKS cluster across availability zones
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Availability Zones ensure high availability by offering independent locations within regions, equipped with their own power, cooling, and networking to ensure applications and data are protected from datacenter-level failures.

Recommendation: Back up Azure Kubernetes Service
Best Practices Guidance: AKS, popular for stateful apps needing backups, can now use Azure Backup to secure clusters and attached volumes through an installed Backup Extension, enabling backup and restore operations via a Backup Vault.

Recommendation: AKS Cluster should have diagnostic settings enabled
Configure user nodepool count
Best Practices Guidance: Configuring the user node pool with at least two nodes is essential for applications needing high availability, ensuring they remain operational and accessible without interruption.

Recommendation: Upgrade Persistent Volumes using in-tree drivers to Azure CSI drivers
Best Practices Guidance: From Kubernetes 1.26, Azure Disk and Azure File in-tree drivers are deprecated in favor of CSI drivers. Existing deployments remain operational but untested; users should switch to CSI drivers for new features and SKUs.

Recommendation: Configure Azure CNI networking for dynamic allocation of IPs or use CNI overlay
Best Practices Guidance: Azure CNI enhances cluster IP and network management, allowing dynamic IP allocation, scalable subnets, direct pod-VNET connectivity, and supports diverse network policies for pods and nodes with Azure Network Policies and Calico, optimizing network efficiency and security

Recommendation: Isolate system and application pods
Best Practices Guidance: AKS assigns the kubernetes.azure.com/mode: system label to nodes in system node pools signaling the preference for system pods should be scheduled there. The CriticalAddonsOnly=true:NoSchedule taint can be added to your system nodes to prohibit application pods from being scheduled on them.

Recommendation: AKS should integrate authentication with AAD (Managed)
Best Practices Guidance: AKS should integrate authentication with AAD (Managed)

Recommendation: AKS should have httpApplicationRouting disabled
Best Practices Guidance: AKS should have httpApplicationRouting disabled

Recommendation: Configure system nodepool count
Best Practices Guidance: The system node pool should be configured with a minimum node count of three to ensure critical system pods are resilient to node outages.

Recommendation: AKS Node Pools should have MaxSurge set
Best Practices Guidance: AKS Node Pools should have MaxSurge set

Recommendation: Enable the cluster auto-scaler on an existing cluster
Best Practices Guidance: The cluster auto-scaler in AKS adjusts node counts based on pod resource needs and available capacity, enabling scaling as per demand to prevent outages.

Recommendation: Enable AKS Monitoring
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Monitor enables real-time health and performance insights for AKS by collecting events, capturing container logs, and gathering CPU/Memory data from the Metrics API. It allows data visualization using Azure Monitor Container Insights, Prometheus, Grafana, or others.

Recommendation: Update AKS tier to Standard or Premium
Best Practices Guidance: Production AKS clusters require the Standard or Premium tier for a financially backed SLA and enhanced node scalability, as the free service lacks these features. Use the Premium tier for mission-critical workloads.

Recommendation: AKS Cluster should be private
Best Practices Guidance: AKS Cluster should be private

Recommendation: AKS should have outbound type set to user defined routing
Best Practices Guidance: AKS should have outbound type set to user defined routing

Recommendation: Expired certificates
Best Practices Guidance: Expired certificates.

Recommendation: For better data path performance enable FastPath on ExpressRoute Connections
Best Practices Guidance: ExpressRoute gateways facilitate network traffic and route exchanges. FastPath enhances on-premises to virtual network data path performance by directing traffic straight to virtual machines, bypassing the gateway for improved resiliency through reduced gateway utilization.

Recommendation: ContainerRegistry should use retention policies
Best Practices Guidance: ContainerRegistry should use retention policies

Recommendation: ContainerRegistry should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: ContainerRegistry should have private endpoints enabled

Recommendation: Enable zone redundancy
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Container Registry's optional zone redundancy enhances resiliency and high availability for registries or replication resources in a specific region by distributing resources across multiple zones.

Recommendation: Enable soft delete policy
Best Practices Guidance: Enabling soft delete preview feature in Azure Container Registry (ACR) allows for the management of deleted artifacts with a specified retention period. Users can list, filter, and restore these artifacts until automatically purged post-retention.

Recommendation: ContainerRegistry should have the Administrator account disabled
Best Practices Guidance: ContainerRegistry should have the Administrator account disabled

Recommendation: Create container registries with geo-replication enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Use Azure Container Registry's geo-replication for multi-region deployments to simplify registry management and minimize latency. It enables serving global customers from local data centers and supports distributed development teams. Regional webhooks can notify of events in replicas.

Recommendation: ContainerRegistry should have diagnostic settings enabled
Disable anonymous pull access
Best Practices Guidance: By default, Azure container registry requires authentication for pull/push actions. Enabling anonymous pull access exposes all content for public read actions. This applies to all repositories, potentially allowing unrestricted access if repository-scoped tokens are used.

Recommendation: Use Premium tier for critical production workloads
Best Practices Guidance: Choose a service tier of Azure Container Registry to meet your performance needs. Premium offers the most bandwidth and highest rate of read and write operations for high-volume deployments. Use Basic to start, Standard for production, and Premium for hyper-scale performance and geo-replication.

Recommendation: Traffic Manager should have diagnostic settings enabled
Traffic manager profiles should have more than one endpoint
Best Practices Guidance: When configuring the Azure traffic manager, provision at least two endpoints to ensure workloads can fail-over to another instance, enhancing reliability and availability.

Recommendation: Ensure endpoint configured to (All World) for geographic profiles
Best Practices Guidance: For geographic routing, traffic is directed to endpoints based on specific regions. If a region fails, without a predefined failover, configuring an endpoint to "All (World)" for geographic profiles can prevent traffic black holes, ensuring service remains available.

Recommendation: Avoid combining Traffic Manager and Front Door
Best Practices Guidance: For most solutions, choose either Azure Front Door for content caching, CDN, TLS termination, and WAF, or Traffic Manager for simple global load balancing.

Recommendation: Traffic Manager Monitor Status Should be Online
Best Practices Guidance: Monitor status should be online to ensure failover for application workload. If Traffic Manager's health shows Degraded, one or more endpoints may also be Degraded.

Recommendation: Traffic Manager without endpoints
Best Practices Guidance: Traffic Manager without endpoints.

Recommendation: Traffic Manager: HTTP endpoints should be monitored using HTTPS
Best Practices Guidance: Traffic Manager: HTTP endpoints should be monitored using HTTPS

Recommendation: Enable Purge protection for Azure App Configuration
Best Practices Guidance: With Purge protection enabled, soft deleted stores can't be purged in the retention period. If disabled, the soft deleted store can be purged before the retention period expires.

Recommendation: Upgrade to App Configuration Standard tier
Best Practices Guidance: SLA is not available for Free tier. Upgrade to the Standard tier to get an SLA of 99.9%

Recommendation: AppConfiguration should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: AppConfiguration should have private endpoints enabled

Recommendation: AppConfiguration should have local authentication disabled
Best Practices Guidance: AppConfiguration should have local authentication disabled

Recommendation: AppConfiguration should have diagnostic settings enabled
App Service should use VNET integration
Best Practices Guidance: App Service should use virtual network integration to securely access resources in Azure virtual networks.

Recommendation: Enable Health check for App Services
Best Practices Guidance: Use Health Check for production workloads. Health check increases your application's availability by rerouting requests away from unhealthy instances, and replacing instances if they remain unhealthy. The Health check path should check critical components of your application.

Recommendation: Enable auto heal for Functions App
Best Practices Guidance: Auto Heal allows you to mitigate your apps when it runs into unexpected situations like HTTP server errors, resource exhaustion, etc. You can configure different triggers based on your need and choose to recycle the app to recover it from a bad state.

Recommendation: App Service should have VNET Route all enabled for VNET integration
Best Practices Guidance: App Service should have VNET Route All enabled to ensure all outbound traffic goes through the integrated virtual network.

Recommendation: App should have diagnostic settings enabled
Logic App should use HTTPS only
Best Practices Guidance: Logic App should enforce HTTPS-only traffic to ensure all communications are encrypted and secure.

Recommendation: Logic App should avoid using Client Affinity
Best Practices Guidance: Logic App should disable Client Affinity (ARR Affinity) for better scalability and reliability.

Recommendation: Function should avoid using Client Affinity
Best Practices Guidance: Function App should disable Client Affinity (ARR Affinity) for better scalability and reliability. Client affinity is generally not needed for Function Apps.

Recommendation: Function should use HTTPS only
Best Practices Guidance: Function App should enforce HTTPS-only traffic to ensure all communications are encrypted and secure.

Recommendation: Logic App should use VNET integration
Best Practices Guidance: Logic App should use virtual network integration to securely access resources in Azure virtual networks.

Recommendation: Deploy to a staging slot
Best Practices Guidance: Create a deployment slot for staging to deploy updates, verify them, and ensure all instances are warmed up before production swap, reducing bad update chances. An LKG slot allows easy rollback to a previous good deployment if issues arise later, enhancing reliability.

Recommendation: App Service should use HTTPS only
Best Practices Guidance: App Service should enforce HTTPS-only traffic to ensure all communications are encrypted and secure.

Recommendation: Function should have VNET Route all enabled for VNET integration
Best Practices Guidance: Function App should have VNET Route All enabled to ensure all outbound traffic goes through the integrated virtual network.

Recommendation: App Service should avoid using Client Affinity
Best Practices Guidance: App Service should disable Client Affinity (ARR Affinity) for better scalability and reliability. Client affinity can cause session stickiness issues in multi-instance deployments.

Recommendation: Logic App should have VNET Route all enabled for VNET integration
Best Practices Guidance: Logic App should have VNET Route All enabled to ensure all outbound traffic goes through the integrated virtual network.

Recommendation: Function should use VNET integration
Best Practices Guidance: Function App should use virtual network integration to securely access resources in Azure virtual networks.

Recommendation: Store configuration as app settings for Web Sites
Best Practices Guidance: Use app settings for configuration and define them in Resource Manager templates or via PowerShell to facilitate part of an automated deployment/update process for improved reliability.

Recommendation: API Connections not related to any Logic App
Best Practices Guidance: API Connections not related to any Logic App.

Recommendation: Deploy zone redundant Container app environments
Best Practices Guidance: To take advantage of availability zones, you must enable zone redundancy when you create a Container Apps environment. The environment must include a virtual network with an available subnet. To ensure proper distribution of replicas, set your app's minimum replica count to three.

Recommendation: Container Apps Environment should have diagnostic settings enabled
Container Apps Environment should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Container Apps Environment should have private endpoints enabled

Recommendation: Front Door WAF Policy without associations
Best Practices Guidance: Front Door WAF Policy without associations.

Recommendation: Resource should have tags
Best Practices Guidance: Resource should have tags applied for proper resource management, cost tracking, and organization. Tags help categorize resources and provide metadata for governance and operational purposes.

Recommendation: Resource should comply with naming conventions
Best Practices Guidance: Resource should comply with naming conventions

Recommendation: Configure diverse VPN Site links to different VPN concentrators on-premises
Best Practices Guidance: Deploying active-active VPN concentrators leverages the High Availability capabilities of the VPN Gateway on Virtual Hubs using a fully-meshed topology with four IPSec tunnels.

Recommendation: A minimum subnet size of /24 is recommended for Application Gateway v2 subnets.
Best Practices Guidance: Application Gateway (Standard_v2 or WAF_v2 SKU) can support up to 125 instances (125 instance IP addresses + 1 private frontend IP configuration + 5 Azure reserved). A minimum subnet size of /24 is recommended.

Recommendation: Application Gateway: Monitor and Log the configurations and traffic
Application Gateways without backend targets
Best Practices Guidance: Application Gateways without backend targets.

Recommendation: Use Health Probes to detect backend availability
Best Practices Guidance: Using custom health probes enhances understanding of backend availability and facilitates monitoring of backend services for any impact.

Recommendation: Plan for backend maintenance by using connection draining
Best Practices Guidance: Using connection draining for backend maintenance ensures graceful removal of backend pool members during updates or health issues. It's enabled via Backend Setting and applies to all members during rule creation.

Recommendation: Ensure Autoscale feature has been enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Application Gateways v2 are always deployed in a highly available fashion with multiple instances by default. Enabling autoscale ensures the service is not reliant on manual intervention for scaling.

Recommendation: Migrate to Application Gateway v2
Best Practices Guidance: Use Application Gateway v2 for built-in features like autoscaling, static VIPs, Azure KeyVault integration for better traffic management and performance, unless v1 is necessary.

Recommendation: Deploy Application Gateway in a zone-redundant configuration
Best Practices Guidance: Deploying Application Gateway in a zone-aware configuration ensures continued customer access to services even if a specific zone goes down, as services in other zones remain available.

Recommendation: Enable zone redundancy in Managed Grafana
Best Practices Guidance: Managed Grafana Standard tier is hosted on a dedicated set of VMs to provide redundancy. With zone redundancy enabled, VMs are spread across availability zones (AZ). Related resources are also configured for AZ. Zone redundancy can only be enabled when creating the Azure Managed Grafana instance.

Recommendation: Azure Managed Grafana should disable public network access
Best Practices Guidance:
Azure Managed Grafana should have public network access disabled
to improve security. Use private endpoints or other secure connectivity methods
instead.

Recommendation: Azure Managed Grafana should have availability zones enabled
Best Practices Guidance:
Azure Managed Grafana should have zone redundancy enabled to ensure
high availability. Zone redundancy provides automatic replication across availability
zones for better resilience.

Recommendation: APIM should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: APIM should have private endpoints enabled

Recommendation: Migrate API Management services to Premium SKU to support Availability Zones
Best Practices Guidance: Upgrading the API Management instance to the Premium SKU adds support for Availability Zones, enhancing availability and resilience by distributing services across physically separate locations within Azure regions.

Recommendation: APIM should only accept a minimum of TLS 1.2
Best Practices Guidance: APIM should only accept a minimum of TLS 1.2

Recommendation: APIM should should not accept weak or deprecated ciphers.
Best Practices Guidance: APIM should should not accept weak or deprecated ciphers.

Recommendation: APIM should use Managed Identities
Best Practices Guidance: APIM should use Managed Identities

Recommendation: Azure API Management platform version should be stv2
Best Practices Guidance: stv1 instances were deprecated on August 31, 2024. If not migrated to stv2 by then, auto-migration will occur. In some cases, due to technical limitations, services may be shut down in March 2025.

Recommendation: Enable Availability Zones on Premium API Management instances
Best Practices Guidance: Zone redundancy for APIM instances ensures the gateway and control plane (Management API, developer portal, Git configuration) are replicated across datacenters in physically separated zones, boosting resilience to zone failures.

Recommendation: APIM should have diagnostic settings enabled
APIM: Renew expiring certificates
Best Practices Guidance: APIM: Renew expiring certificates

Recommendation: Fabric Capacity should use Fabric (F) SKU tier for production workloads
Best Practices Guidance:
Fabric Capacity should use the Fabric (F) SKU tier for production
workloads to ensure proper performance and support levels.

Recommendation: Fabric Capacity should be in Active state
Best Practices Guidance:
Fabric Capacity should be in Active state to be available for
use. Paused capacities are not available for processing workloads.

Recommendation: Fabric Capacity should have administrators configured
Best Practices Guidance:
Fabric Capacity should have administrators configured to ensure
proper management and security oversight of the capacity.

Recommendation: Replicate your Image Templates to a secondary region
Best Practices Guidance: The Azure Image Builder service lacks availability zones support. Replicating Image Templates to a secondary region will enable the build of new images in secondary region.

Recommendation: Ensure ODAA clusters are in Available state under normal operations
Best Practices Guidance:
ODAA Vm Clusters can be in a number of states during their lifecycle (Available, Failed, Maintenance In Progress, Provisioning, Terminated, Terminating and Updating).
Ensure that the ODAA clusters are in Available state under normal operations.

Recommendation: Resolve issues with Private Endpoints in non Succeeded connection state
Best Practices Guidance: A private endpoint has two custom properties, static IP address and the network interface name, which must be set at creation. If not in Succeeded state, there may be issues with the endpoint or associated resource.

Recommendation: Private Endpoints not connected to any resource
Best Practices Guidance: Private Endpoints not connected to any resource.

Recommendation: Subnets without Connected Devices or Delegation
Best Practices Guidance: Subnets without Connected Devices or Delegation.

Recommendation: CosmosDB should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: CosmosDB should have private endpoints enabled

Recommendation: CosmosDB: disable write operations on metadata resources (databases, containers, throughput) via account keys
Best Practices Guidance: CosmosDB: disable write operations on metadata resources (databases, containers, throughput) via account keys

Recommendation: Configure continuous backup mode
Best Practices Guidance: Cosmos DB's backup is always on, offering protection against data mishaps. Continuous mode allows for self-serve restoration to a pre-mishap point, unlike periodic mode which requires contacting Microsoft support, leading to longer restore times.

Recommendation: Evaluate multi-region write capability
Best Practices Guidance: Multi-region writes enable high availability but require careful consistency and conflict resolution. Using Bounded Staleness is an anti-pattern, as replication lag increases latency and coordination overhead. This setup undermines scalability, impacting performance and availability.

Recommendation: Enable availability zones
Best Practices Guidance: When availability zones are configured, Azure Cosmos DB intelligently distributes the 4 replicas of your data across all available zones. It ensures that your Azure Cosmos DB can withstand an outage in one availability zone and remain fully operational throughout.

Recommendation: Enable service-managed failover for multi-region accounts with single write region
Best Practices Guidance: Cosmos DB boasts high uptime and resiliency. Even so, issues may arise. With Service-Managed failover, if a region is down, Cosmos DB automatically switches to the next available region, requiring no user action.

Recommendation: Configure at least two regions for high availability
Best Practices Guidance: Enable a secondary region in Cosmos DB for higher SLA without downtime. Simple as pinning a location on a map. For Strong consistency, configure at least three regions for write availability in case of failure.

Recommendation: CosmosDB should have local authentication disabled
Best Practices Guidance: CosmosDB should have local authentication disabled

Recommendation: CosmosDB should have diagnostic settings enabled
Monitor health for ExpressRoute gateway
Best Practices Guidance: Set up monitoring and alerts for ExpressRoute Gateway. Create alert rule for ensuring promptly response to critical events such as exceeding packets per second, exceeding BGP routes prefixes, Gateway overutilization and high frequency in route changes.

Recommendation: Avoid using ExpressRoute circuits for VNet to VNet communication
Best Practices Guidance: Avoid using ExpressRoute circuits for VNet to VNet communication. Use Virtual WAN to connect VNets in different regions. This feature is enabled at the ExpressRoute circuit level but reflected in the Virtual WAN resource.

Recommendation: Logic App should limit access to Http Triggers
Best Practices Guidance: Logic App should limit access to Http Triggers

Recommendation: Logic App should have diagnostic settings enabled
Azure FrontDoor should have diagnostic settings enabled
Migrate Stream Analytics jobs to StandardV2 SKU
Best Practices Guidance: Configure Autoscale to allow your job to dynamically change the allocated number of Streaming Units (SU) based on load, metrics, and/or schedule.

Recommendation: Run jobs in your own dedicated Stream Analytics cluster for increased reliability and security
Best Practices Guidance: Stream Analytics cluster (dedicated) offers more reliable performance guarantees. All the jobs running on your cluster belong only to you. You can also have access to important features like private endpoints, Auto-Scaling, Vnet Support, etc.

Recommendation: ContainerInstance should use private IP addresses
Best Practices Guidance:
Azure Container Instances should use private IP addresses instead
of public IPs for enhanced security and to follow network security best practices.

Recommendation: ContainerInstance should have availability zones enabled
Best Practices Guidance:
Azure Container Instances should have availability zones enabled
to ensure high availability and protect against datacenter failures.

Recommendation: Shared disks should only be enabled in clustered servers
Best Practices Guidance: Azure shared disks let you attach a disk to multiple VMs at once for deploying or migrating clustered applications, suitable only when a disk is shared among VM cluster members.

Recommendation: Use Azure Disks with Zone Redundant Storage for higher resiliency and availability
Best Practices Guidance: Azure disks offers a zone-redundant storage (ZRS) option for workloads that need to be resilient to an entire zone being down. Due to the cross-zone data replication, ZRS disks have higher write latency when compared to the locally-redundant option (LRS), so make sure to benchmark your disks.

Recommendation: Managed Disks with 'Unattached' state
Best Practices Guidance: Managed Disks with 'Unattached' state.

Recommendation: Load Balancer should have diagnostic settings enabled
Use NAT Gateway instead of Outbound Rules for Production Workloads
Best Practices Guidance: Outbound rules for Standard Public Load Balancer involve manual port allocation for backend pools, limiting scalability and risk of SNAT port exhaustion. NAT Gateway is recommended for its dynamic scaling and secure internet connectivity.

Recommendation: Ensure Standard Load Balancer is zone-redundant
Best Practices Guidance: In regions with Availability Zones, assigning a zone-redundant frontend IP to a Standard Load Balancer ensures continuous traffic distribution even if one availability zone fails, provided other healthy zones and backend instances are available to receive the traffic.

Recommendation: Load Balancers with empty backend address pools
Best Practices Guidance: Load Balancers with empty backend address pools.

Recommendation: Use Standard Load Balancer SKU
Best Practices Guidance: Selecting Standard SKU Load Balancer enhances reliability through availability zones and zone resiliency, ensuring deployments withstand zone and region failures. Unlike Basic, it supports global load balancing and offers an SLA.

Recommendation: Ensure the Backend Pool contains at least two instances
Best Practices Guidance: Deploying Azure Load Balancers with at least two instances in the backend prevents a single point of failure and supports scalability. Pairing with Virtual Machine Scale Sets is advised for optimal scale building.

Recommendation: Network Security Groups not attached to any network interface or subnet
Best Practices Guidance: Network Security Groups not attached to any network interface or subnet.

Recommendation: Monitor changes in Network Security Groups with Azure Monitor
Best Practices Guidance: Create Alerts with Azure Monitor for operations like creating or updating Network Security Group rules to catch unauthorized/undesired changes to resources and spot attempts to bypass firewalls or access resources from the outside.

Recommendation: NSG should have diagnostic settings enabled
Monitor gateway for Site-to-site v-Hub's VPN gateway
Best Practices Guidance: Set up monitoring and alerts for v-Hub's VPN Gateway. Create alert rule for ensuring promptly response to critical events such as packet drop counts, BGP status, Gateway over utilization. Mission Critical workloads should use dual ExpressRoutes instead of VPN.

Recommendation: Deploy Azure Firewall across multiple availability zones
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Firewall offers different SLAs depending on its deployment; in a single availability zone or across multiple, potentially improving reliability and performance.

Recommendation: Monitor Azure Firewall metrics
Best Practices Guidance: Monitor Azure Firewall for overall health, processed throughput, and outbound SNAT port usage. Get alerted before limits impact services. Consider NAT gateway integration with zonal deployments; note limitations with zone redundant firewalls and secure virtual hub networks.

Recommendation: Azure Firewall should have diagnostic settings enabled
Deploy Network Watcher in all regions where you have networking services
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Network Watcher offers tools for monitoring, diagnosing, viewing metrics, and managing logs for IaaS resources. It helps maintain the health of VMs, VNets, application gateways, load balancers, but not for PaaS or Web analytics.

Recommendation: Configure Network Watcher Connection monitor
Best Practices Guidance: Improves monitoring for Azure and Hybrid connectivity

Recommendation: Redis should enforce TLS >= 1.2
Best Practices Guidance: Redis should enforce TLS >= 1.2

Recommendation: Redis should have diagnostic settings enabled
Redis should not enable non SSL ports
Best Practices Guidance: Redis should not enable non SSL ports

Recommendation: Enable zone redundancy for Azure Cache for Redis
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Cache for Redis offers zone redundancy in Premium and Enterprise tiers, using VMs across multiple Availability Zones to ensure greater resilience and availability.

Recommendation: Consider upgrading legacy storage accounts to v2 storage accounts
Best Practices Guidance: General-purpose v2 accounts are recommended for most storage scenarios offering the latest features or the lowest per-gigabyte pricing. Legacy accounts like Standard general-purpose v1 and Blob Storage aren't advised by Microsoft but may fit specific scenarios.

Recommendation: Storage Account should use HTTPS only
Best Practices Guidance: Storage Account should require secure transfer (HTTPS) to ensure all data transfers are encrypted in transit.

Recommendation: Storage Account should enforce TLS >= 1.2
Best Practices Guidance: Storage Account should enforce minimum TLS version 1.2 to ensure secure communications using modern encryption protocols.

Recommendation: Ensure that storage accounts are zone or region redundant
Best Practices Guidance: Redundancy ensures storage accounts meet availability and durability targets amidst failures, weighing lower costs against higher availability. Locally redundant storage offers the least durability at the lowest cost.

Recommendation: Storage should have diagnostic settings enabled
Storage Account should have immutable storage versioning enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Storage Account should have immutable storage with versioning enabled to protect against accidental or malicious data deletion or modification.

Recommendation: Azure Synapse Workspace should establish network segmentation boundaries
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Synapse Workspace should have managed virtual network enabled to establish proper network segmentation boundaries.

Recommendation: Azure Synapse Workspace should have diagnostic settings enabled
Azure Synapse Workspace should disable public network access
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Synapse Workspace should have public network access disabled to prevent unauthorized access from the internet.

Recommendation: Azure Synapse Workspace should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Azure Synapse Workspace should have private endpoints configured to secure access and prevent exposure to the public internet.

Recommendation: Migrate from Basic to Standard Virtual WAN
Best Practices Guidance: Basic SKU is not recommended for critical workloads. Standard SKU provides important features Inter-hub and VNet-to-VNet transiting through the virtual hub, ExpressRoute, VPN and Point-to-Site Gateways, ability to deploy Azure Firewalls and NVAs.

Recommendation: Monitor health for v-Hubs
Best Practices Guidance: Set up monitoring and alerts for v-Hubs. Create alert rule for ensuring promptly response to changes in BGP status and Data processed by v-Hubs.

Recommendation: Service should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Service should have private endpoints enabled

Recommendation: Service should have local authentication disabled
Best Practices Guidance: Service should have local authentication disabled

Recommendation: Service should have diagnostic settings enabled
Use replica sets for resiliency or geolocation in Microsoft Entra Domain Services
Best Practices Guidance:
To improve the resiliency of a Microsoft Entra Domain Services managed domain, or deploy to additional geographic locations close to your applications, you can use replica sets.
You can add a replica set to any peered virtual network in any Azure region that supports Domain Services.

Recommendation: Use at least the Enterprise SKU
Best Practices Guidance: You need to use a minimum of Enterprise SKU for your managed domain to support replica sets.

Recommendation: NAT Gateways not attached to any subnet
Best Practices Guidance: NAT Gateways not attached to any subnet.

Recommendation: NAT Gateway should have diagnostic settings enabled
Enable zone redundancy for Azure SQL Database to achieve high availability and resiliency
Best Practices Guidance: By default, Azure SQL Database premium tier provisions multiple copies within the same region. For geo redundancy, databases can be set as Zone Redundant, distributing copies across Azure Availability Zones to maintain availability during regional outages.

Recommendation: SQL Database should have diagnostic settings enabled
Use Zone-redundant or Geo-zone-redundant Backup storage redundancy
Best Practices Guidance: Configuring zone redundancy option for backups copies your backup file synchronously across three Azure availability zones in the primary region. If Geo is selected, then it copies your data asynchronously three times to a single physical location in the paired secondary region.

Recommendation: Use Redirect connection type to accelerate application access
Best Practices Guidance: Redirect mode enables direct connectivity to the instance bypassing the local gateway component and resulting in improved latency and throughput. Redirect mode applies to the VNet-local endpoint only, while the public and private endpoint will always operate in Proxy connection mode.

Recommendation: Enable zone redundancy for Azure SQL Managed Instance to improve high availability and resiliency
Best Practices Guidance: Azure SQL Managed Instance offers built-in availability by deploying multiple replicas in the same zone. For higher availability, use a zone-redundant configuration that spreads replicas across three Azure availability zones, each with independent power, cooling, and networking.

Recommendation: DDoS protection without protected resources
Best Practices Guidance: DDoS protection without protected resources.

Recommendation: Enable AZ support in AI Search by configuring multiple replicas to your search service
Best Practices Guidance: Availability zones are used when adding multiple replicas to your search service. Each replica is assigned to a different zone in the region. If there are more replicas than zones, they are distributed as evenly as possible across the available zones.

Recommendation: Azure AI Search should disable public network access
Best Practices Guidance: Azure AI Search should disable public network access

Recommendation: Azure AI Search should have private enpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Azure AI Search should have private enpoints enabled

Recommendation: Azure AI Search should have diagnostic settings enabled
Azure Analysis Service should have diagnostic settings enabled
The Admin State of both Links of an ExpressRoute Direct should be in Enabled state
Best Practices Guidance: In Azure ExpressRoute Direct, the "Admin State" indicates the administrative status of layer 1 links, showing if a link is enabled or disabled, effectively turning the physical port on or off.

Recommendation: Ensure ExpressRoute Direct is not over-subscribed
Best Practices Guidance: Provisioning ExpressRoute circuits on a 10-Gbps or 100-Gbps ExpressRoute Direct resource up to 20-Gbps or 200-Gbps is possible but not recommended for resiliency. If an ExpressRoute Direct port fails, and circuits are using full capacity, the remaining port won't handle the extra load.

Recommendation: Do not use free tier
Best Practices Guidance: In a production scenario, the IoT Hub tier should not be Free because the Free tier does not provide the necessary Service Level Agreement.

Recommendation: Use Device Provisioning Service
Best Practices Guidance: Device Provisioning Service (DPS) enables easy redistribution of IoT devices for scaling and availability, allowing devices to be reassigned and not bound to specific IoT Hub instances. Devices in IoT Hubs using DPS should be verified for DPS utilization.

Recommendation: Disabled Fallback Route
Best Practices Guidance: Using message routing for custom endpoints in IoT Hub, messages might not reach these destinations if specific conditions are unmet. A default route ensures all messages are received, but disabling this safety net risks leaving some messages undelivered.

Recommendation: Network Interfaces not attached to any resource
Best Practices Guidance: Network Interfaces not attached to any resource.

Recommendation: Monitor health for v-Hub's Point-to-Site VPN gateways
Best Practices Guidance: Set up monitoring and alerts for Point-to-Site VPN gateways. Create alert rule for ensuring promptly response to critical events such as Gateway over utilization, connection count limits and User VPN route limits. Mission Critical workloads should use dual ExpressRoutes instead of VPN.

Recommendation: Deploy VMSS with Flex orchestration mode instead of Uniform
Best Practices Guidance: Deploying even single instance VMs into a scale set with Flexible orchestration mode future-proofs applications for scaling and availability. This mode guarantees high availability (up to 1000 VMs) by distributing VMs across fault domains in a region or within an Availability Zone.

Recommendation: Deploy VMSS across availability zones with VMSS Flex
Best Practices Guidance: When creating VMSS, implement availability zones as a protection measure for your applications and data against the rare event of datacenter failure.

Recommendation: Enable Automatic Repair Policy on Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets
Best Practices Guidance: Enabling automatic instance repairs in Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets enhances application availability through a continuous health check and maintenance process.

Recommendation: Enable Azure Virtual Machine Scale Set Application Health Monitoring
Best Practices Guidance: Monitoring application health in Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets is crucial for deployment management. It supports rolling upgrades such as automatic OS-image upgrades and VM guest patching, leveraging health monitoring for upgrading.

Recommendation: Configure VMSS Autoscale to custom and configure the scaling metrics
Best Practices Guidance: Use custom autoscale for VMSS based on metrics and schedules to improve performance and cost effectiveness, adjusting instances as demand changes.

Recommendation: Disable Force strictly even balance across zones to avoid scale in and out fail attempts
Best Practices Guidance: Microsoft advises disabling strictly even VM instance distribution across Availability Zones in VMSS to improve scalability and flexibility, noting that uneven distribution may better serve application load demands despite the potential trade-off in resilience.

Recommendation: Enable Predictive autoscale and configure at least for Forecast Only
Best Practices Guidance: Predictive autoscale utilizes machine learning to efficiently manage and scale Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets by forecasting CPU load through historical usage analysis, ensuring timely scale-out to meet demand.

Recommendation: Use Ephemeral OS Disks for AKS VMSS Node Pools
Best Practices Guidance: Ephemeral OS disks on AKS offer lower read/write latency due to local attachment, eliminating the need for replication seen with managed disks. This enhances performance and speeds up cluster operations such as scaling or upgrading due to quicker re-imaging and boot times.

Recommendation: Web Pub Sub should have private endpoints enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Web Pub Sub should have private endpoints enabled

Recommendation: Web Pub Sub should have availability zones enabled
Best Practices Guidance: Web Pub Sub should have availability zones enabled

Recommendation: Web Pub Sub should have diagnostic settings enabled
Resource Groups without resources
Best Practices Guidance: Resource Groups without resources.
